name: OmniRoute
description: A self-hosted AI gateway exposing a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint across 231+ providers — around 90 with free tiers — with automatic fallback, cost/speed/quality routing, token compression, and ready-made hookups for Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, and other coding agents.
why: The 'never pay for tokens' pitch made it explode, and it's a revealing lens on the free-tier arbitrage subculture around coding agents — including the risks that come with it.
useCases: A local gateway consolidating API keys
audience: Individual developers and tinkerers minimizing LLM spend

setup.requirements: Node or Docker; API keys for the providers you enable; point your agent at the endpoint

limitations: Routing your prompts and code through many third-party 'free' providers raises real privacy and terms-of-service questions — free-tier access via a gateway may violate upstream terms. Marketing claims (95% token compression) deserve skepticism, and the project leans hard on one maintainer.
take: Genuinely useful infrastructure wrapped in a value proposition that works right up until the providers decide it doesn't. Route accordingly.
